_comment: The source of truth for the use-case gallery. Add an entry here + a usecases/<slug>/ folder (README + replay.html + example-input.json [+ sample-output.txt]), then run `python tools/build_gallery.py` to regenerate index.html. Every new successful use case gets added this way.

usecases.title: Triage — a support inbox that reads itself
usecases.tagline: Takes a GitHub issue: works out what it is, how urgent, who owns it, drafts a reply, checks it, and waits for a human.
usecases.meta: shared: classify · rank · verify · act  ·  domain: route · respond

usecases.title: Content — a writer that does its own research
usecases.tagline: Takes a topic: pulls sources, picks an angle, writes a short post in a set voice, checks it's on-brand, and stages it. Reuses the same specialists as triage — only write is new.
usecases.meta: shared: fetch · classify · rank · verify · act  ·  domain: write

usecases.title: Refine — an editor that won't ship slop
usecases.tagline: Drafts, grades itself against a strict bar, and rewrites and re-checks until it passes — then waits for a human. The loop is the point; watch tokens flow back through write ↔ verify.
usecases.meta: the control-flow loop — a real run failed twice, revised, passed on the third
